preset_description: Dark = decision signal. Emphasis accumulates toward conversion — trust anchors and commitment moments are dark, top-of-funnel is light.

accent_tokens._note: Brand primary CTA color. Global — not per-preset. Two variants: button on a light/neutral section vs. on a dark section.

beats.name: Orientation
beats.rationale: Establishes immediate context for trial users: reminds them what they've been using, reframes the product around the value they've already experienced, and signals this page is about taking the next step.

beats.name: Evidence
beats.rationale: Validators need proof before committing. Presenting concrete metrics, usage stats, or case study data early satisfies their need for rational justification and preempts skepticism before the pricing conversation.

beats.name: Credibility
beats.rationale: Reinforces the evidence with social proof — customer logos, testimonials, or trust signals. For a Validator at the conversion stage, seeing that peers have made this same decision de-risks the commitment.

beats.name: Decision
beats.rationale: Required at the conversion funnel stage. Presents pricing and plan comparison so trial users can evaluate their options clearly. Positioned after proof beats so the Validator feels informed rather than pressured.

beats.name: Conversion
beats.rationale: Final commitment prompt — a clear, singular CTA to upgrade. Placed immediately before Recovery per rules, and streamlined for a conversion-stage audience that already understands the product from their trial experience.

beats.name: Recovery
beats.rationale: Provides secondary paths for users not ready to convert — extend trial, talk to sales, or revisit documentation. Catches exit intent and preserves the relationship for Validators who need more time.

sections.name: Navigation
sections.rationale: Global navigation — wayfinding and persistent CTA for the session.

sections.name: Pricing
sections.rationale: Required at the conversion funnel stage. Presents pricing and plan comparison so trial users can evaluate their options clearly. Positioned after proof beats so the Validator feels informed rather than pressured.

sections.name: CTA
sections.rationale: Final commitment prompt — a clear, singular CTA to upgrade. Placed immediately before Recovery per rules, and streamlined for a conversion-stage audience that already understands the product from their trial experience.

sections.name: Footer
sections.rationale: Page close — brand mark, minimal navigation, and legal.

page.reasoning: minimal variant for conversion-focused pages — strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only. This aligns with the conversion funnel stage goal to maximize focus on the primary conversion action.

Validator variant includes description and longer feature list, supporting trial users who need substantive information to make conversion decisions. highlighted variant for recommended plan drives upgrade intent.

slots.description: The full intelligence layer — resolution engine, IA planner, multi-variant generation, palette map, and Next.js preview UI. No subscription, no vendor dependency.
features: IA planner + multi-variant generation (Mover, Validator, Explorer)
Three palette presets — funnel, archetype, feature-emphasis
Token dashboard with editable palette map
Lifetime access — no subscription, no lock-in

page.reasoning: Validator variant includes subhead and trust_signal (e.g., 'No credit card required'), which addresses trial user concerns and reduces friction for conversion.

slots.headline: Your trial showed you what MODE does.
slots.subhead: One purchase gives you the full resolution engine, every preset, and lifetime access — running in your codebase in under an hour.
slots.trust_signal: One-time payment. No subscription. No runtime dependency. Yours to fork and extend.

page.reasoning: with-cta variant recaptures departing users on key conversion pages — use on pricing and signup flows, directly supporting the conversion goal for trial users.
